findings.issue: The draft goes beyond the allowed §4.4 hand-off into §4.5 by naming upcoming continuity and derivative work for \(\ln x\). The brief allows only the forward fence that \(e^x\) is strictly increasing/invertible and that the next section builds \(\ln x\).
findings.evidence: “The next section names that inverse \(\ln x\), establishes its continuity from the strict monotonicity just proved, and computes \(\frac{d}{dx} \ln x\) rigorously …”
findings.suggestion: Trim the close to the permitted hand-off: \(e^x\) is strictly increasing, hence one-to-one/invertible on its range, so §4.5 builds \(\ln x\).

summary: The proof ladder Theorem 4.10 → Rolle → MVT → Corollary is mathematically sound, including the Rolle Case 2 correction, MVT auxiliary function, strict/non-strict monotonicity split, and all three examples. The only blocking issue is the §4.5 scope leak in the closing paragraph.
